{{Infobox country at games
| NOC               = 
| NPC               = BRA
| IAAF              = 
| CGA               = 
| NF                = 
| country           = Brazil
| year              = 2026
| games             = Winter Paralympics
| games_link        = 
| flag              = 
| flagcaption       = [[Flag of Brazil]]
| alt               = 
| oldcode           = 
| NOCname           = 
| NPCname           = [[Brazilian Paralympic Committee]]
| CGAname           = 
| NFname            = 
| fedname           = 
| website           = 
| location          = Milan & Cortina d'Ampezzo, Italy
| start_date        = {{Start date|2026|3|6|df=y}}
| end_date          = {{End date|2026|3|15|df=y}}
| date              = 
| competitors       = 8
| competitors_men   = 5
| competitors_women = 3
| sports            = 3
| events            = 
| flagbearer        = 
| flagbearer_open   = [[Cristian Ribera]]<br />[[Aline Rocha]]
| flagbearer_close  = [[André Barbieri]]
| coaches           = 
| rank              = 23
| officials         = 
| gold              = 
| silver            = 1
| bronze            = 
| appearances       = auto
| app_begin_year    = 2014
| app_interval      = 4
| summerappearances = 
| winterappearances = 
| youthappearances  = 
| seealso           = 
| previous          = 
| next              = 
}}
[[Brazil]] will compete at the [[2026 Winter Paralympics]] in [[Milan]] & [[Cortina d'Ampezzo]], Italy, which will take place between 6–15 March 2026.<ref>[https://www.olympics.com/en/milano-cortina-2026/paralympic-games/results/hubs/individuals?noc=BRA]</ref>

Para cross-country skier & para-biathlete [[Aline Rocha]] and para cross-country skier [[Cristian Ribera]] were the country's opening ceremony flagbearers. Meanwhile, the para-snowboarder [[André Barbieri]] was the country's flagbearer during the closing ceremony.

On 10 March 2026, Brazil won the first medal in their [[Winter Paralympic Games|Winter Paralympics]] history, with [[Cristian Ribera]]'s silver medal in the [[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's sprint classical#Sitting|Men's sprint - sitting]]. It was also the first medal for a [[Tropical nations at the Winter Olympics#Winter Paralympic Games|tropical nation]] and [[South America]] at the Winter Games.<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 March 2026 |title=Cristian Ribera conquista prata histórica para o Brasil nas Paralimpíadas de Inverno|url=https://ge.globo.com/paralimpiadas-de-inverno/noticia/2026/03/10/cristian-ribera-conquista-prata-historica-para-o-brasil-nas-paralimpiadas-de-inverno.ghtml/ |access-date=10 March 2026 |website=Globo Esporte |language=pt-BR}}</ref>

==Medallists==
{{main|2026 Winter Paralympics medal table}}
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size: 95%;"
|-
!Medal
!Name
!Sport
!Event
!Date
|-
| {{silver medal}} || {{Sortname|Cristian|Ribera}} || [[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics|Para cross-country skiing]] || [[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's sprint classical#Sitting|Men's sprint, sitting]] || {{dts|10 March}}
|}

==Competitors==
The following is the list of number of competitors participating at the Games per sport/discipline.

{|class="wikitable sortable" style=text-align:center
! width=150|Sport
! width=55|Men
! width=55|Women
! width=55|Total
|-
|align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics|Para biathlon]]
| rowspan=2|4 || rowspan=2|2 || rowspan=2|6
|-
|align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics|Para cross-country skiing]]
|-
| align=left|[[Para snowboard at the 2026 Winter Paralympics|Para snowboard]]
| 1 || 1 || 2
|-
! Total || 5 || 3 || 8
|}

==Para biathlon==
{{Main|Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics}}
Brazil has qualified four para-biathletes for the following Paralympics events.

;Men
{| class=wikitable style="font-size:90%"
! rowspan=2|Athlete
! rowspan=2|Class
! rowspan=2|Event
! colspan=3|Qualification
! colspan=3|Final
|-
! Missed shots
! Time
! Rank
! Missed shots
! Time
! Rank
|-align=center
| align=left rowspan=3|[[Robelson Lula]]
| align=left rowspan=3|LW12
| align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's individual#Sitting|Individual, sitting]]
|colspan=3{{n/a}}
|10 (1+3+4+2)
|48:54.9
|26
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's sprint#Sitting|Sprint, sitting]]
|colspan=3{{n/a}}
|4 (2+2)
|24:27.0
|21
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's sprint pursuit#Sitting|Sprint pursuit, sitting]]
|4 (2+2)
|10:54.7
|24 '''Q'''
|2 (0+2)
|13:56.6
|21
|-align=center
| align=left rowspan=3|[[Guilherme Rocha]]
| align=left rowspan=3|LW12
| align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's individual#Sitting|Individual, sitting]]
|colspan=3{{n/a}}
|3 (1+0+2+0)
|42:30.9
|16
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's sprint#Sitting|Sprint, sitting]]
|colspan=3{{n/a}}
|0 (0+0)
|22:48.9
|16
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's sprint pursuit#Sitting|Sprint pursuit, sitting]]
|1 (0+1)
|9:57.7
|19 '''Q'''
|2 (1+1)
|12:55.9
|15
|}

;Women
{| class=wikitable style="font-size:90%"
! rowspan=2|Athlete
! rowspan=2|Class
! rowspan=2|Event
! colspan=3|Qualification
! colspan=3|Final
|-
! Missed shots
! Time
! Rank
! Missed shots
! Time
! Rank
|-align=center
| align=left rowspan=3|[[Elena de Sena|Elena {{nowrap|de Sena}}]]
| align=left rowspan=3|LW12
| align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's individual#Sitting|Individual, sitting]]
|colspan=3{{n/a}}
|6 (0+1+2+3)
|54:36.8
|11
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's sprint#Sitting|Sprint, sitting]]
|colspan=3{{n/a}}
|1 (1+0)
|27:43.2
|14
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's sprint pursuit#Sitting|Sprint pursuit, sitting]]
|0 (0+0)
|12:12.2
|12 '''Q'''
|3 (2+1)
|17:42.2
|12
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Aline Rocha]]
| align=left|LW10.5
| align=left|[[Para biathlon at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's sprint#Sitting|Sprint, sitting]]
|colspan=3{{n/a}}
|2 (0+2)
|23:50.6
|7
|}

==Para cross-country skiing==
{{Main|Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics}}
Brazil has qualified six para cross-country skiers for the following Paralympics events.

;Individual
{| class=wikitable style="font-size:90%"
! rowspan=2|Athlete
! rowspan=2|Class
! rowspan=2|Event
! colspan=2|Qualification
! colspan=2|Semifinal
! colspan=2|Final
|- style="font-size:95%"
! Time
! Rank
! Time
! Rank
! Time
! Rank
|-align=center
| align=left rowspan=3|[[Wellington da Silva (skier)|Wellington da Silva]]
| align=left rowspan=3|LW8
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's sprint classical#Standing|Men's sprint classical, standing]]
|2:48.29
|19
|colspan=4|Did not advance
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's 10 kilometre classical#Standing|Men's 10 km classical, standing]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|33:19.9
|18
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's 20 kilometre freestyle#Standing|Men's 20 km freestyle, standing]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|52:54.0
|25
|-align=center
| align=left rowspan=3|[[Robelson Lula]]
| align=left rowspan=3|LW12
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's sprint classical#Sitting|Men's sprint, sitting]]
|2:25.01
|20
|colspan=4|Did not advance
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's 10 kilometre classical#Sitting|Men's 10 km, sitting]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|26:46.3
|15
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's 20 kilometre freestyle#Sitting|Men's 20 km, sitting]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|1:01:07.3
|22
|-align=center
| align=left rowspan=3|'''[[Cristian Ribera]]'''
| align=left rowspan=3|LW11
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's sprint classical#Sitting|Men's sprint, sitting]]
|2:08.22	
|1 '''Q'''
|2:28.7
|1 '''Q'''
|2:29.6
|{{silver02}}
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's 10 kilometre classical#Sitting|Men's 10 km, sitting]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|24:31.1
|5
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's 20 kilometre freestyle#Sitting|Men's 20 km, sitting]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|53:40.8
|5
|-align=center
| align=left rowspan=3|[[Guilherme Rocha]]
| align=left rowspan=3|LW12
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's sprint classical#Sitting|Men's sprint, sitting]]
|2:22.41
|18
|colspan=4|Did not advance
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's 10 kilometre classical#Sitting|Men's 10 km, sitting]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|26:34.3
|14
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's 20 kilometre freestyle#Sitting|Men's 20 km, sitting]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|58:49.4
|19
|-align=center
| align=left rowspan=3|[[Elena de Sena]]
| align=left rowspan=3|LW12
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's sprint classical#Sitting|Women's sprint, sitting]]
|2:57.52
|16
|colspan=4|Did not advance
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's 10 kilometre classical#Sitting|Women's 10 km, sitting]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|33:33.8
|16
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's 20 kilometre freestyle#Sitting|Women's 20 km, sitting]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|1:19:04.9
|14
|-align=center
| align=left rowspan=3|[[Aline Rocha]]
| align=left rowspan=3|LW10.5
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's sprint classical#Sitting|Women's sprint, sitting]]
|2:37.56
|3 '''Q'''
|3:05.0
|2 '''Q'''
|3:21.0
|5
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's 10 kilometre classical#Sitting|Women's 10 km, sitting]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|28:37.3
|5
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Para cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's 20 kilometre freestyle#Sitting|Women's 20 km, sitting]]
|colspan=4{{n/a}}
|1:01:30.2
|5
|}

;Relay
{| class=wikitable style="font-size:90%"
! Athletes
! Event
! Time
! Rank
|-
|[[Cristian Ribera]]<br />[[Aline Rocha]]<br />[[Wellington da Silva (skier)|Wellington da Silva]]<br />[[Wellington da Silva (skier)|Wellington da Silva]]
| [[Cross-country skiing at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Mixed 4 × 2.5 kilometre relay|4 × 2.5 km mixed relay]]
| align="center"|27:00.5
| align="center"|7
|}

==Para snowboard==
{{Main|Para snowboard at the 2026 Winter Paralympics}}
Brazil has qualified two para-snowboarders for the following Paralympics events. One of them qualified for Milan-Cortina 2026, by virtue in the Paralympic rankings in their respective class. And also secured one place under the bipartite invitation (one of the quotas not used by another continent).

;Banked slalom
{| class=wikitable style="font-size:90%"
|-
! Athlete
! Event
! Run 1
! Run 2
! Best
! Rank
|-align=center
| align=left|[[André Barbieri]]
| align=left|[[Snowboarding at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Men's banked slalom#Banked slalom SB-LL1|Men's banked slalom, SB-LL1]]
|<s>1:16.31</s>
|1:11.86
|1:11.86
|13
|-align=center
| align=left|[[Vitoria Machado]]
| align=left|[[Snowboarding at the 2026 Winter Paralympics – Women's banked slalom#Banked slalom SB-LL2|Women's banked slalom, SB-LL2]]
|<s>1:21.68</s>
|1:16.42
|1:16.42
|12
|}

==See also==
*[[Brazil at the Paralympics]]
*[[Brazil at the 2026 Winter Olympics]]

==References==
{{reflist}}

{{NPCs at the 2026 Winter Paralympics}}
{{Authority control}}

[[Category:Nations at the 2026 Winter Paralympics]]
[[Category:Brazil at the Winter Paralympics|2026]]
[[Category:2026 in Brazilian sport|Winter Paralympics]]
[[Category:Brazil–Italy sports relations|2026-03-06]]
